text.title: capacity-bottleneck-checklist Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the capacity-bottleneck-checklist methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Apply only to a single instance of role-software-architect/Capacity + cost-modelling exercise. Never batch unrelated decisions through one pass.
rule.rationale: Batching turns a 1-decision artefact into a debate club; each instance gets its own record so the rationale is traceable.

rule.statement: Every input field has a declared type and source. Free-form prose without a source citation is rejected.
rule.rationale: Untyped inputs let LLMs hallucinate plausible-but-wrong values.

rule.statement: The output names a single accountable owner (handle / email / org role). No 'team' or 'we' as owner.
Output carries semver-style version and last_reviewed date. Stale (>90 days, no review) records are flagged on read.
rule.rationale: Methodology outputs decay; treating them as immutable is a defect.

rule.statement: Headroom floor: >=20% spare capacity at the chosen horizon. If headroom < 20%, escalate before commit.
rule.rationale: Utilization above 80% drives latency exponentially; 20% slack is the practical floor.

rule.statement: Skip when the input does not satisfy the Applies-If preconditions or another methodology owns this output shape.
rule.rationale: Prevents duplicate work and wrong-tool application.
